files.patch:
@@ -818,7 +818,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-adgv","title":"Task: Add --robot-mail-check flag","description":"## Overview

Add `--robot-mail-check` flag for AI orchestrators to check agent inboxes via Agent Mail integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the **harmonized** robot mode patterns for tool bridges:
- **Naming**: `--robot-mail-check` (no inline value)
- **Project param**: `--project=PROJ` (standard modifier flag)
- **Agent param**: `--agent=ID` (optional filter modifier)
- **Shared modifiers**: `--limit=N`, `--offset=N`, `--since=DATE`, `--until=DATE` (global flags)
- Consistent with `--robot-xf-search --query=Q`, `--robot-dcg-check --command=CMD`

## Command Interface

```bash
# Check all agent inboxes for a project
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject

# Check specific agent inbox
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --agent=cc_1

# Include full message bodies
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --include-bodies

# Limit and paginate results
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --limit=10 --offset=20

# Only urgent messages
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --urgent-only

# Filter by read status
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --status=unread

# Filter by date range
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30

# Check specific thread
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --thread=TKT-api-design

# Verbose output with extra details
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --verbose
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "project": "myproject",
  "agent": "cc_1",
  "filters": {
    "status": "all",
    "urgent_only": false,
    "thread": null,
    "since": null,
    "until": null
  },
  "unread": 2,
  "urgent": 1,
  "total_messages": 45,
  "offset": 0,
  "count": 10,
  "messages": [
    {
      "id": "msg-123",
      "from": "agent-cc-2",
      "to": "cc_1",
      "subject": "Re: API endpoint design",
      "preview": "I think we should use...",
      "body": null,
      "thread_id": "TKT-api-design",
      "importance": "normal",
      "read": false,
      "timestamp": "2026-01-22T10:25:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "has_more": true,
  "_agent_hints": {
    "suggested_action": "Reply to agent-cc-2 about API design",
    "unread_summary": "2 unread messages, 1 urgent",
    "next_offset": 10,
    "pages_remaining": 4,
    "oldest_unread": "2026-01-22T08:15:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "error": "Agent Mail not available",
  "error_code": "TOOL_NOT_FOUND",
  "hint": "Start Agent Mail server: am start"
}
```

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default | Scope |
|------|-------------|---------|-------|
| `--project=PROJ` | Project to check (required) | - | **Required** |
| `--agent=ID` | Filter to specific agent inbox | all | Optional |
| `--thread=ID` | Filter to specific thread | all | Optional |
| `--status=S` | Filter by read status: read, unread, all | all | Optional |
| `--include-bodies` | Include full message bodies | false | Optional |
| `--urgent-only` | Only show urgent messages | false | Optional |
| `--verbose` | Include extra details in output | false | Global shared |
| `--limit=N` | Max messages to return | 20 | Global shared |
| `--offset=N` | Skip first N messages (pagination) | 0 | Global shared |
| `--since=DATE` | Filter to messages after date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |
| `--until=DATE` | Filter to messages before date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |

## Code Location
- internal/robot/mail_check.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-mail-check` flag registered in root.go (bool flag)
- [ ] `--project=` required modifier for project name
- [ ] `--agent=` optional filter selects specific inbox
- [ ] `--thread=` optional filter for specific thread
- [ ] `--status=` optional filter for read/unread/all
- [ ] `--include-bodies` includes full message content
- [ ] `--limit=N` caps messages (default 20) - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--offset=N` for pagination - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--since=DATE` filters by start date - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--until=DATE` filters by end date - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--urgent-only` filters to urgent messages only
- [ ] `--verbose` includes extra details in output
- [ ] JSON output includes timestamp field
- [ ] JSON output includes filters object showing active filters (including until)
- [ ] JSON output includes total_messages count for pagination
- [ ] JSON output includes `count` field (messages in this response)
- [ ] JSON output includes has_more boolean
- [ ] Each message includes `read` boolean field
- [ ] Each message includes `to` field
- [ ] Returns inbox status with unread/urgent counts
- [ ] Message previews included (truncated to ~100 chars)
- [ ] `body` field is null unless --include-bodies
- [ ] Agent hints provide actionable suggestions including next_offset and pages_remaining
- [ ] Exit codes follow convention (0/1/2)
- [ ] Clear error when Agent Mail not available (exit code 2, error_code: TOOL_NOT_FOUND)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/mail_check_test.go)

| Test Case | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `TestMailCheckFlagParsing` | Parse all flags: --project, --agent, --thread, --status, --include-bodies, --limit, --offset, --since, --until, --urgent-only, --verbose |
| `TestMailCheckProjectRequired` | Error when --project is missing |
| `TestMailCheckAgentFilter` | Filter messages to specific agent |
| `TestMailCheckThreadFilter` | Filter messages to specific thread |
| `TestMailCheckStatusFilter` | Filter by read/unread/all status |
| `TestMailCheckStatusValidation` | Reject invalid status values |
| `TestMailCheckIncludeBodies` | Include full message bodies when flag set |
| `TestMailCheckBodyNullByDefault` | Body is null when --include-bodies not set |
| `TestMailCheckLimitEnforced` | Limit caps messages correctly |
| `TestMailCheckOffsetApplied` | Offset skips correct number of messages |
| `TestMailCheckSinceFilter` | Filter messages by start date |
| `TestMailCheckUntilFilter` | Filter messages by end date |
| `TestMailCheckDateRangeValidation` | Error if --until is before --since |
| `TestMailCheckUrgentOnly` | Filter to urgent messages only |
| `TestMailCheckVerboseMode` | Include extra details when --verbose set |
| `TestMailCheckAPIResponseParsing` | Parse Agent Mail API response correctly |
| `TestMailCheckMessagePreview` | Truncate message preview to ~100 chars |
| `TestMailCheckReadField` | Include read boolean in each message |
| `TestMailCheckToField` | Include to field in each message |
| `TestMailCheckJSONSerialization` | Output matches schema with all required fields |
| `TestMailCheckTimestampIncluded` | Response includes RFC3339 timestamp |
| `TestMailCheckFiltersObject` | Response includes filters object with all filters |
| `TestMailCheckTotalMessages` | Calculate total_messages correctly |
| `TestMailCheckCountField` | Calculate count (messages in response) correctly |
| `TestMailCheckHasMore` | Set has_more when total_messages > offset + count |
| `TestMailCheckUnreadCount` | Calculate unread count correctly |
| `TestMailCheckUrgentCount` | Calculate urgent count correctly |
| `TestMailCheckAgentHints` | Generate actionable agent hints with next_offset and pages_remaining |
| `TestMailCheckMissingAgentMail` | Error with TOOL_NOT_FOUND when Agent Mail unavailable |
| `TestMailCheckInvalidProject` | Error for non-existent project |
| `TestMailCheckEmptyInbox` | Return success with empty messages array |
| `TestMailCheckExitCodes` | Map errors to correct exit codes (0/1/2) |

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_mail.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# [E2E-MAIL] Robot Mail Check End-to-End Tests
# =============================================================================
# Tests the --robot-mail-check command for Agent Mail inbox integration.
# Exit codes: 0=all passed, 1=test failed, 77=skipped (Agent Mail not running)
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-MAIL]"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
SKIPPED=0

log_info()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [INFO]  $*"; }
log_pass()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [PASS]  $*"; ((PASSED++)); }
log_fail()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [FAIL]  $*"; ((FAILED++)); }
log_skip()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [SKIP]  $*"; ((SKIPPED++)); }
log_debug() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [DEBUG] $*"; }

cleanup() {
    log_info "Test summary: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_info "Starting robot mail-check E2E tests"
log_info "Test timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prerequisite Check
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

# Check if Agent Mail is running (default port 8765)
if ! curl -s http://localhost:8765/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_skip "Agent Mail not running on localhost:8765 - skipping all tests"
    exit 77
fi

log_info "Agent Mail health check passed"

if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
    log_fail "jq is required for tests"
    exit 1
fi

# Use a test project name
TEST_PROJECT="ntm-e2e-test"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-001: Check project inbox
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-001: Check project inbox"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.project == "'"$TEST_PROJECT"'"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("timestamp") and has("total_messages") and has("filters") and has("count")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "MAIL-001: Project inbox check works with all required fields"
        else
            log_fail "MAIL-001: Missing required fields (timestamp, total_messages, filters, or count)"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-001: Project not echoed in response"
    fi
else
    # Check if it's a project-not-found error (acceptable for test project)
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.error_code == "PROJECT_NOT_FOUND"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-001: Project check works (test project not found is expected)"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-001: Check did not succeed"
    fi
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-002: Check specific agent inbox
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-002: Check specific agent inbox"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --agent=cc_1 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.agent == "cc_1"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-002: Agent filter accepted"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-002: Agent not set in response"
    fi
else
    # Accept error response as valid test (project may not exist)
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-002: Agent filter syntax accepted (error due to test data)"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-002: Request failed unexpectedly"
    fi
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-003: Missing --project parameter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-003: Missing --project parameter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check 2>&1) || true
EXIT_CODE=$?
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"
log_debug "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.error' 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "project.*required"; then
        log_pass "MAIL-003: Missing project correctly rejected"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-003: Error message should mention project is required"
    fi
elif echo "$RESULT" | grep -qi "project.*required"; then
    log_pass "MAIL-003: Missing project correctly rejected (stderr)"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-003: Should require --project"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-004: Include bodies flag
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-004: Include bodies flag"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --include-bodies 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-004: Include bodies flag accepted"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-004: Include bodies flag parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-005: Limit and offset parameters
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-005: Limit and offset parameters"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --limit=5 --offset=10 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    MSG_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.messages | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    OFFSET=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.offset' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.count' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    if [[ "$MSG_COUNT" -le 5 ]] && [[ "$OFFSET" -eq 10 ]]; then
        if [[ "$COUNT" -ge 0 ]]; then
            log_pass "MAIL-005: Limit, offset, and count respected"
        else
            log_fail "MAIL-005: Missing count field"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-005: Limit or offset not applied correctly"
    fi
else
    log_pass "MAIL-005: Limit/offset flags accepted (error due to test data)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-006: Urgent only flag
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-006: Urgent only flag"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --urgent-only 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.filters.urgent_only == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-006: Urgent only flag reflected in filters"
    else
        log_pass "MAIL-006: Urgent only flag accepted"
    fi
else
    log_fail "MAIL-006: Urgent only flag parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-007: Status filter - unread
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-007: Status filter (unread)"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --status=unread 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.filters.status == "unread"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-007: Status filter reflected in response"
    else
        log_pass "MAIL-007: Status filter accepted"
    fi
else
    log_fail "MAIL-007: Status filter parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-008: Invalid status value
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-008: Invalid status value"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --status=invalid 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-008: Invalid status rejected"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-008: Should reject invalid status value"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-009: Thread filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-009: Thread filter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --thread=TKT-test-123 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.filters.thread == "TKT-test-123"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-009: Thread filter reflected in response"
    else
        log_pass "MAIL-009: Thread filter accepted"
    fi
else
    log_fail "MAIL-009: Thread filter parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-010: Since date filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-010: Since date filter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --since=2024-01-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-010: Since date filter accepted"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-010: Since date filter parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-011: Until date filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-011: Until date filter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --until=2024-12-31 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.filters.until == "2024-12-31"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-011: Until date filter reflected in response"
    else
        log_pass "MAIL-011: Until date filter accepted"
    fi
else
    log_fail "MAIL-011: Until date filter parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-012: Date range (since and until)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-012: Date range filter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-012: Date range accepted"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-012: Date range parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-013: Invalid date range (until before since)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-013: Invalid date range"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --since=2024-06-01 --until=2024-01-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-013: Invalid date range rejected"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-013: Should reject --until before --since"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-014: Unread and urgent counts
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-014: Unread and urgent counts"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("unread") and has("urgent") and has("total_messages")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        UNREAD=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.unread' 2>/dev/null)
        URGENT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.urgent' 2>/dev/null)
        TOTAL=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.total_messages' 2>/dev/null)
        log_pass "MAIL-014: Counts present (unread: $UNREAD, urgent: $URGENT, total: $TOTAL)"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-014: Missing unread, urgent, or total_messages counts"
    fi
else
    log_pass "MAIL-014: Counts check skipped (test project may not exist)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-015: Agent hints with next_offset and pages_remaining
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-015: Agent hints with next_offset and pages_remaining"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("_agent_hints")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '._agent_hints | has("next_offset") and has("pages_remaining")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "MAIL-015: Agent hints include next_offset and pages_remaining"
        else
            log_pass "MAIL-015: Agent hints present (some pagination fields optional)"
        fi
    else
        log_pass "MAIL-015: No agent hints (optional field)"
    fi
else
    log_pass "MAIL-015: Agent hints check skipped"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-016: Messages array always present
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-016: Messages array always present"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("messages") and (.messages | type == "array")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-016: Messages array present"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-016: Missing messages array"
    fi
else
    log_pass "MAIL-016: Messages check skipped (test data)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-017: Message has read and to fields
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-017: Message has read and to fields"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true and (.messages | length) > 0' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.messages[0] | has("read") and has("to")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-017: Message has read and to fields"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-017: Message missing read or to field"
    fi
else
    log_pass "MAIL-017: Field check skipped (no messages)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-018: Exit codes follow standard
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-018: Exit code verification"

# Test exit code 1 for missing required flag
ntm --robot-mail-check >/dev/null 2>&1 || EXIT_CODE=$?
if [[ "${EXIT_CODE:-0}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    log_pass "MAIL-018: Exit code 1 for missing required flag"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-018: Expected exit code 1, got ${EXIT_CODE:-0}"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-019: has_more and count fields
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-019: has_more and count fields"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --limit=1 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("has_more") and has("count")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "MAIL-019: has_more and count fields present"
    else
        log_fail "MAIL-019: Missing has_more or count field"
    fi
else
    log_pass "MAIL-019: has_more/count check skipped"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-020: Verbose mode
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-020: Verbose mode"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --verbose 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-020: Verbose mode accepted"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-020: Verbose mode parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test MAIL-021: Combined options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test MAIL-021: Combined options"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-mail-check --project="$TEST_PROJECT" --agent=cc_1 --status=unread --limit=5 --urgent-only --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-12-31 --verbose 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true or .success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "MAIL-021: Combined options accepted"
else
    log_fail "MAIL-021: Combined options parsing failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final Summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "========================================="
log_info "Final Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
log_info "========================================="

if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
fi

log_info "All robot mail-check E2E tests passed!"
exit 0
```

## API Harmonization Notes

This bead was updated to follow the canonical robot mode tool bridge pattern:
- Changed from `--robot-mail-check=PROJECT` to `--robot-mail-check --project=PROJ`
- Added `--thread=ID` for filtering to specific thread
- Added `--status=read|unread|all` for read status filtering
- Added `--offset=N` for pagination
- Added `--since=DATE` for time filtering (start date)
- Added `--until=DATE` for time filtering (end date)
- Added `--verbose` for extra output details
- Added `read` field to each message
- Added `to` field to each message
- Added `filters` object showing active filters (including until)
- Added `total_messages` for pagination awareness
- Added `count` field (messages in current response)
- Added `has_more` boolean
- Enhanced `_agent_hints` with `next_offset` and `pages_remaining`
- This matches the harmonized pattern used by JFP, CASS, XF, DCG, and other tool bridges","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-ak7b","title":"Master Integration Test Suite: Full NTM System Flow","description":"## Comprehensive Integration Test Suite

### Purpose
End-to-end integration test that exercises all major NTM features working together in a realistic **system flow** (not the workflow DSL).

### Test Flow
1. **Project Initialization**
   - ntm init testproject
   - Verify directories/configs

2. **Template-based Spawn**
   - ntm spawn testproject --template=code-review
   - Verify agents/personas

3. **CM Context Loading**
   - Verify CM memories loaded
   - Verify guards injected

4. **Task Assignment**
   - Create test beads
   - ntm assign testproject
   - Verify tasks distributed

5. **File Reservation**
   - Agents edit files
   - Verify reservations + conflicts

6. **Agent Communication**
   - Agents send messages
   - Verify delivery + threading

7. **Context Monitoring**
   - Fill agent context
   - Verify alerts displayed

8. **Cost Tracking**
   - Verify cost displayed
   - Verify per-agent breakdown

9. **Staggered Operations**
   - Send staggered prompt
   - Verify timing

10. **Handoff**
    - Kill agent
    - Handoff to replacement
    - Verify context transferred

11. **Prompt History**
    - Verify prompts recorded
    - Test replay

12. **Session Summarization**
    - ntm summarize testproject
    - Verify output

13. **Output Archive**
    - ntm kill testproject --archive
    - Verify CASS integration

14. **Effectiveness Scoring**
    - Complete tasks
    - Verify scores updated

15. **Session Recovery**
    - Simulate crash
    - ntm recover testproject
    - Verify restoration

### Detailed Logging Requirements
- Log each phase start/end with timestamps
- Log assertions with expected vs actual
- Generate test report with pass/fail summary
- On failure: capture state, scrollback, logs

### Test Report Format
```json
{
  "test_id": "integration-001",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T10:30:00Z",
  "duration_seconds": 120,
  "phases": [
    {"name": "init", "status": "pass", "duration_ms": 500}
  ],
  "summary": {"total": 15, "passed": 15, "failed": 0}
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 15 test phases pass
2. Test report JSON format validated
3. Detailed logging for each phase
4. Cleanup removes all test artifacts
5. Test duration under 30 minutes
6. Test runner script: scripts/integration_master_test.sh
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any  {"id":"bd-aklq2","title":"Feature: CLI Commands - ntm ensemble command family","description":"# Feature: CLI Commands - Ensemble-first interface

**Users pick goals, not modes.** The primary CLI interface is ensemble selection.

## Primary Commands

### ntm ensemble [ensemble-name] [problem]
Run a named ensemble:
```bash
ntm ensemble project-diagnosis 'What are the main issues with this codebase?'
ntm ensemble idea-forge 'What features should we add next?'
ntm ensemble spec-critique 'Review the API specification'
ntm ensemble safety-risk 'Analyze security concerns'
```

### ntm ensemble list
Show available ensembles with descriptions:
```
Available Ensembles:

project-diagnosis   Systems analysis, red-team, failure modes
idea-forge          Divergent brainstorming, innovation
spec-critique       Requirements review, edge cases
safety-risk         Threat modeling, compliance
architecture-review Multi-perspective arch analysis
tech-debt-triage    Dependency analysis, prioritization
bug-hunt            Multi-angle bug detection
root-cause          Causal + counterfactual analysis
strategic-planning  Long-term planning under uncertainty

Use 'ntm ensemble [name] --help' for details
```

### ntm ensemble [name] --help
Show ensemble details (modes, strategy, typical budget).

### ntm ensemble status [session]
Check running ensemble status (uses existing status patterns).

### ntm ensemble synthesize [session]
Trigger synthesis for completed ensemble.

## Advanced Commands (Power Users)

### Custom modes
```bash
# Explicit mode list by id or code
ntm ensemble --modes=A1,B13,F43 --question 'Analyze the auth system'
```
- If any **advanced tier** modes are included, require explicit flag: `--allow-advanced`.
- Print a warning when advanced modes are used.

### ntm ensemble modes
Browse the mode catalog:
```bash
ntm ensemble modes                    # List core modes (default)
ntm ensemble modes --all              # Include advanced
ntm ensemble modes --category formal  # Filter by category
ntm ensemble modes --verbose          # Full mode details
```

### ntm ensemble presets
Alias for `ntm ensemble list` (shared implementation).

## Flags (CLI)
Common flags across ensemble commands:
```
--agents string      Override agent distribution (cc=3,cod=2,gmi=1)
--assignment string  round-robin|affinity|explicit
--allow-advanced     Explicit opt-in for advanced/experimental modes
--tier string        core|advanced|experimental|all (list/modes only)
--format string      table|json|yaml (list/modes/presets/status/synthesize)
--output string      Write synthesis to file (synthesize only)
--no-questions       Skip targeted questions (use auto-context only)
--no-cache           Bypass context pack cache
--budget-total int   Override total tokens
--budget-per-agent int Override per-agent cap
```

## Automation (Robot API)
Automation uses **robot flags**, not a `--robot` flag on the CLI:
- `ntm --robot-ensemble=SESSION`
- `ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION`
- `ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION`
- `ntm --robot-ensemble-modes`
- `ntm --robot-ensemble-presets`

## Files
- internal/cli/ensemble.go - Main ensemble command
- internal/cli/ensemble_list.go - List ensembles
- internal/cli/ensemble_status.go - Status command
- internal/cli/ensemble_synthesize.go - Synthesis trigger
- internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go - Mode browser","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:25:56.341552291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:54:40.422400162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:54:40.422142647Z","close_reason":"Core CLI commands implemented: ntm ensemble [preset] [question], spawn, status, synthesize, suggest, stop, resume, rerun-mode, provenance, clean-checkpoints. Mode browsing via ntm modes list/explain. Robot API flags available. Remaining: E2E tests (bd-3vkzo), tab completion (bd-1e5jh), presets list (bd-6mls6).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aklq2","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-aklq2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kthb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-aklq2","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-avlbg","title":"DCG: Add --robot-dcg-status command","description":"Add robot mode command to report DCG status and configuration.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-dcg-status
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "dcg": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true,
    "version": "1.2.3",
    "binary_path": "/usr/local/bin/dcg",
    "config": {
      "audit_log": "~/.ntm/dcg_audit.log",
      "allow_override": true,
      "custom_blocklist_count": 0,
      "custom_whitelist_count": 0
    },
    "stats": {
      "commands_checked": 1234,
      "commands_blocked": 5,
      "last_blocked": {
        "command": "rm -rf /",
        "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
        "pane": "agent-1"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Add to robot mode command registry
2. Gather DCG state from config and runtime stats
3. Format as JSON according to schema
4. Include recent blocked commands from audit log

## Code Location
- internal/robot/dcg_status.go
- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

## Testing
- Call with DCG enabled and available
- Call with DCG enabled but not available
- Call with DCG disabled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:17.536129348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:00:34.381259683Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:00:34.381184612Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/robot/dcg_status.go with tests, --robot-dcg-status flag in root.go handler. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-avlbg","depends_on_id":"bd-1jiuu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-avlbg","depends_on_id":"bd-1m6sw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-avlbg","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-avlbg","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ayjn","title":"EPIC: Smart Staggering for Rate Limit Avoidance","description":"**Smart Staggering for Rate Limit Avoidance EPIC**

## What It Does
Intelligent timing of agent spawns and prompts to avoid hitting provider rate limits.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Simultaneous spawns can trigger rate limits
- After: Staggered launches prevent limit hits
- Critical for large swarms

## Features
- Configurable stagger delay (default 30s)
- Per-provider timing awareness
- Adaptive delays based on rate limit history

## Success Criteria

1. Default 30s stagger between agent spawns
2. Per-provider configurable delays
3. Adaptive delays based on rate limit tracker
4. ntm spawn --stagger DURATION flag works
5. Rate limit events reduce stagger automatically
6. Unit tests for stagger logic (80% coverage)
7. E2E tests verify no rate limits hit with staggering","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:26:07.836870787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:38:41.469683129Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ayjn","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"related","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
